title: Simple Member North Carolina  LLC operating agreement

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Simple Member North Carolina  LLC operating agreement

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Operating Agreement for Simple Member LLC in North Carolina

This Operating Agreement (“Agreement”) is entered into on [date] by and between [Member Name] (“Member”) and any other members that may join in the future.

Formation of the LLC

The Members have formed a limited liability company (“LLC”) under the laws of the State of North Carolina. The name of the LLC is [LLC Name].

Purpose of the LLC

The purpose of the LLC is to engage in any lawful business activity within the State of North Carolina.

Member Ownership

The Member is the sole owner of the LLC.

Capital Contributions

The Member has contributed [amount] of capital to the LLC.

Profits and Losses

The Member will receive all profits and will bear all losses of the LLC.

Management

The Member shall manage the LLC.

Right of First Refusal

The Member shall have a right of first refusal with respect to any transfer of membership interests by the LLC.

Indemnification

The LLC shall indemnify the Member for any liabilities or expenses incurred by the Member in connection with the LLC.

Amendments

This Agreement may be amended only with the written consent of the Member.

Governing Law

This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of North Carolina. 

Entire Agreement

This Agreement represents the entire understanding and agreement between the parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof, and supersedes all prior negotiations, discussions, agreements, and understandings between them. 

By signing below, the Member acknowledges and agrees to the terms and conditions of this Agreement.

Member Signature: ________________________________

Date: _______________________

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: add protection provisions and clauses and tax handling

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Protection Provisions and Clauses:

1. Limited Liability: The LLC, as a separate legal entity, shall provide limited liability protection to the Member(s) for any business debts and obligations incurred by the LLC, beyond the amount of capital contributions.

2. Indemnification: The LLC shall indemnify the Member(s) against any liabilities or expenses incurred due to any action taken on behalf of the LLC, except in the case of intentional misconduct or recklessness.

3. Insurance: The LLC shall maintain adequate insurance coverage, including general liability insurance and property insurance, to protect against any potential risks that the LLC may face.

4. Liability of the Member(s): The Member(s) shall not be held liable for any debts or obligations incurred by the LLC beyond the amount of capital contributions.

Tax Handling:

1. Tax Classification: The LLC shall be classified as a disregarded entity for federal tax purposes, meaning that the Member(s) shall report the LLC's income and losses on their individual income tax returns.

2. Tax Deductions: The LLC shall be entitled to deduct business expenses incurred in the course of its operations from its taxable income. The Member(s) shall not be entitled to deduct any expenses related to the LLC on their individual tax returns.

3. Tax Payments: The LLC shall pay any state and federal taxes owed on its taxable income. The Member(s) shall be responsible for paying any taxes on their individual income related to their share of the LLC's profits.

4. Tax Returns: The LLC shall file an annual tax return with the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and the state of North Carolina. The Member(s) shall receive a copy of the LLC's Schedule K-1, which reports their share of the LLC's income and losses for the year.

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text:
Corporate Governance:

1. The LLC shall be governed by its members.

2. The Member(s) shall make all major decisions related to the LLC, including but not limited to, approval of budgets, hiring of employees, investment decisions, and amendments to this operating agreement.

3. The Member(s) shall have equal voting rights, and all decisions shall be made by a majority vote.

4. The LLC shall maintain accurate and up-to-date records of all meetings and decisions of the Member(s).

Non-Compete Clause:

1. The Member(s) agree that they shall not engage in any competitive activities with the LLC during the term of this Agreement.

2. The Non-Compete Clause shall apply geographical location or any other terms mutually agreed upon by the Member(s).

Book Access and Audits:

1. The Member(s) shall have access to all the books and records of the LLC at all times.

2. The Member(s) shall have the right to conduct audits of the LLC's books and records at any time, upon reasonable notice to the other Member(s).

Buy-Sell Agreement:

1. The Member(s) agree to have a Buy-Sell agreement in place to provide for a smooth change in ownership of the LLC in the event of the death, disability, retirement, or voluntary or involuntary withdrawal of a Member.

2. The Buy-Sell agreement shall identify the buyout price and payment terms, as well as the method of valuation.

Purpose of the LLC:

1. The LLC's purpose is to engage in [insert business purpose].

Outline of the Roles and Responsibilities of Each Member:

1. [Insert Member Name] shall be responsible for [insert responsibilities].

2. [Insert Member Name] shall be responsible for [insert responsibilities].

Change in Ownership:

1. The Member(s) shall provide written notice to the other Member(s) in the event of a change in ownership of their membership interest.

2. The remaining Member(s) shall have the right of first refusal to purchase the departing Member's membership interest.

Member Ownership Percentage:

1. The Member(s) percent ownership is [insert percentage] of the LLC.

Dissolution of LLC

1. The LLC shall be dissolved upon the death, disability, retirement, or voluntary or involuntary withdrawal of a Member, unless otherwise provided for in this Agreement. 

2. In the event of dissolution, the assets and liabilities of the LLC shall be distributed in accordance with North Carolina law.

Profit and Losses Distribution:

1. The LLC's profits and losses shall be distributed to the Member(s) in proportion to their percentage of ownership.

The Decision-Making Process:

1. The Member(s) shall make all major decisions related to the LLC, and all decisions shall be made by a majority vote.

2. In the event of a tie vote, the Member(s) shall seek the services of a mediator or arbitrator to help resolve the impasse.
